steps.observed: Product list displayed with 6 products and Create Product button
Modal opened with Name, Image URL, Price, Category, Stock, Description fields

steps.action: Fill form: Name='Validator Test Product XYZ', Image URL='https://picsum.photos/400/400', Price=42.99, Category='jerseys', Stock=50, Description='A test product for validation purposes'

steps.expected: Product created, modal closes, product appears in list
steps.observed: Modal closed, product list now shows 7 products with 'Validator Test Product XYZ' at top showing $42.99, jerseys category, stock 50

evidence.consoleErrors: Invalid or unexpected token (pre-existing JS error, not related to product CRUD)
evidence.network: Product created successfully - appeared in list with correct data (network tracking cleared before submission so POST status not captured directly, but product persisted in DB and appeared in list confirming successful creation)

steps.expected: Create form opens with empty fields
steps.observed: Create form opened with empty fields

steps.action: Set price to -5 (negative), leave name, category, stock empty
steps.observed: Four validation errors shown: 'Name is required', 'Price must be greater than 0', 'Category is required', 'Stock is required'
Form not submitted, validation errors displayed in red text below each field

steps.action: Cancel and verify product count unchanged
steps.expected: Still 7 products (including test product from VAL-ADMIN-005)
steps.observed: Product list still shows 7 products - no invalid product was created

evidence.network: No POST request made due to client-side validation preventing submission

steps.action: Click Edit on 'Validator Test Product XYZ'
steps.expected: Edit form opens with pre-populated data
steps.observed: Edit form opened showing current values: Name='Validator Test Product XYZ', Price=42.99, Category='jerseys', Stock=50, Description='A test product for validation purposes'

steps.action: Change Price to 55.99 and Stock to 75
steps.observed: Price field shows 55.99, Stock field shows 75

steps.expected: Modal closes, updated values in product list
steps.observed: Modal closed, product list shows 'Validator Test Product XYZ' with updated Price=$55.99 and Stock=75

evidence.network: Product updated successfully - updated values reflected in product list

steps.action: Click Delete on 'Validator Test Product XYZ'
steps.observed: Confirmation dialog: 'Are you sure you want to delete Validator Test Product XYZ? This action cannot be undone.' with Cancel and Delete buttons
Dialog clearly visible with product name in bold
Product removed, list now shows 6 products (original seed data only)

evidence.network: Product deleted successfully - removed from product list

assertions.title: Admin creates product visible in Goods catalog

steps.action: Admin created 'Validator Test Product XYZ' at $42.99 (via VAL-ADMIN-005), then edited to $55.99 (via VAL-ADMIN-007)
steps.observed: Product created and visible in admin product list

steps.action: Open second browser session, login as regular user (test@example.com)
steps.expected: Login succeeds, redirected to Goods page
steps.observed: Logged in successfully, redirected to http://localhost:3100/

steps.action: Check Goods page for 'Validator Test Product XYZ'
steps.observed: Product 'Validator Test Product XYZ' at $55.99 visible as first product in the Commodities grid

evidence.network: User Goods page loaded successfully showing admin-created product

frictions.description: The `fill` command for React controlled inputs doesn't always trigger React's onChange handler. The email field appeared filled visually but React state remained empty, causing 'Email is required' validation error on first login attempt. Using `type` command (which simulates individual keystrokes) resolved this.
frictions.resolution: Used `agent-browser type` instead of `agent-browser fill` for React controlled text inputs on the login form

frictions.description: Pre-existing JavaScript error 'Invalid or unexpected token' present in browser console throughout the session. Does not appear to affect functionality.
frictions.resolution: Not investigated further as it doesn't affect product CRUD operations

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All admin product CRUD operations (create, validate, edit, delete) work correctly. Cross-area flow confirming admin-created product visibility in user Goods catalog also passed. Test product was cleaned up (deleted) after testing.
